titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert Giroux Papers
titlestmt.author: Tina Evans

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday May 19, 2025 at 23:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection contains correspondence to and from Robert Giroux, along with related correspondence between others.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Peter Harrington on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund and Ruth Stephan Fund, 2024.
Purchased from Appledore Books, Black Dog Books, and Chatham Bookseller, LLD on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into four series: I. Correspondence, 1929-2002. II. Writings, 1935-1993. III. Photographs, 1914-2005. IV. Professional papers, 1921-2001.

bioghist.head: ROBERT GIROUX (1914-2008)
bioghist.p: Robert Giroux was an American book editor and publisher. He was born April 8, 1914, in Jersey City, New Jersey. He attended Columbia University, before going on to work for Columbia Broadcasting Network, Harcourt Brace & Company, Farrar, Straus & Company (later Farrar, Straus & Giroux).

Giroux worked with many prominent authors including John Berryman, Elizabeth Bishop, T. S. Eliot, Robert Lowell, Thomas Merton, and others. Giroux also served as the president of the National Board of Review of Motion Pictures.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert Giroux Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence to and from Robert Giroux, along with related correspondence between others; writings of authors such as T.S. Elliot, Robert Lowell, and Elizabeth Bishop, represented by Robert Giroux including typescripts, proofs, and galleys; photographs of Robert Giroux and others; and other papers relating to Giroux and publishing.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Robert Giroux Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rrights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by last name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains letters to and from Robert Giroux, as well as third party letters retained in the files of Robert Giroux.

did.unittitle: Selected letters from Vincent to Brooke on the occasion of Brooke Astor's one hundredth birthday

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a group of letters from William Vincent Astor to Brooke Astor, once housed in a commemorative folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains John Berryman , by Saul Bellow,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to and from T. S. Eliot, along with correspondence relating to T. S. Eliot.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains sympathy letters sent to Robert Giroux upon the death of T. S. Eliot.

did.unittitle: Farrar, Straus and Cudahy, Inc. Publishers
Manhattanville College of the Sacred Heart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains Walker Percy's Truth by Eudora Welty.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by last name

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, drafts, galleys, and other related materials.

did.unittitle: Auden W. H.: Louis Macneice a memorial address

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a booklet privately printed for Faber and Faber London.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains The Columbia Review, Vol. XVI, No. 3.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript (photocopy), inscribed to Robert Giroux by John Berryman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains The Columbia Review, Volume XVII, No. 3.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected typescript.

did.unittitle: John Berryman's NBA acceptance speech, March 1969

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscript and typescript drafts of acceptance speech.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescript notes, corrected, for an unidentified work.

did.unittitle: Our sins are more than we can bear

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected proof.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains The Columbia Review, Volume XVII, No. 2.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains five copies of a Christmas card.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected proof.

did.unittitle: Edgar Allan Poe & the juke-box unfinished poems, drafts, and fragments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript by Elizabeth Bishop, edited by Alice Quinn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript, accompanied with a corrected typescript note by Robert Giroux.

did.unittitle: Was it in his hand?: photocopy
Cummings, E. E.: Boys I mean are not refined

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript sent to Robert Giroux.

did.unittitle: Blurb for The Antiphon A tragedy in verse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript, accompanied by a note from Faber & Faber.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the fragment of a corrected typescript (photostat).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected galley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a typescript photostat, pages 1-44.

did.unittitle: Knowledge and experience in the philosophy of F. H. Bradly: book jacket

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ccontains two copies of the book jacket and is accompanied by a memorandum from Farrar, Straus & Giroux, Inc.

did.unittitle: Poems written in early youth: introduction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected proof.

did.unittitle: Poems written in early youth. The death of Saint Narcissus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected, photocopied proof.

did.unittitle: Eliots, and the T. S. Eliot collection at Harvard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an offprint from the Harvard Library Bulletin, Volume XXXVI, Number 3, Summer 1988, with an annotation from March 1900 and inscribed to Robert Giroux from Donald Gallup, 1990 May 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an a reprint from The Yale University Library Gazette, Volume 67, Number 1-2, inscribed to Robert Giroux from Donald Gallup, 1992 December 15.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains corrected typescript drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The file contains a typescript draft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected proof.

did.unittitle: Getting started: Jean Stafford's early novels

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected typescript accompanied by a memorandum from Farrar, Straus & Giroux, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected and signed typescript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain a mockup and a printed version inscribed to "Charles" from Robert Giroux.

scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected typescript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a corrected, signed typescript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
